    <item>
      <media:content url="https://wwr-pro.s3.amazonaws.com/logos/0076/1823/logo.gif" type="image/png"/>
      <title>MailerLite: Graphic Designer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>California</state>
      <skills>Graphic Design</skills>
      <category>Design</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0076/1823/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> USA
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.mailerlite.com/company-values">https://www.mailerlite.com/company-values</a>
</p>


<div class="container">
<div class="lg:px-20 grid lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6 lg:gap-20 xl:gap-32 items-start">
<div>
<div class="text text-xl leading-relaxed space-y-10 lg:mt-2.5">
<p>MailerLite is one of the fastest-growing email marketing services. We help more than 1 million businesses around the world stay in touch with their customers. Today, we are a team of more than 170 dreamers, adventurers, and world travelers passionate about what we do and what we believe in. And we are ready for another talented person to join the party.</p>
<p>We're looking for a&nbsp;<strong>Graphic Designer</strong>&nbsp;who can help communicate our brand across different marketing channels, including ads, social media, and our website. This is a marketing-focused design role — partnering closely with our marketing, development, and product teams to create visuals that make an impact.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>


<div class="container space-y-20">
<div class="lg:px-20">
<h2 class="mb-6 dark:text-white">&nbsp;</h2>
<h2 class="mb-6 dark:text-white">Why MailerLite?</h2>
<div class="text text-xl mt-4 max-w-screen-lg leading-relaxed dark:text-white/70">
<p>Wondering why we think you’ll love working for MailerLite? Here are our favorite 6 reasons!</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
</div>
<div class="lg:px-20 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-x-14 gap-y-16">
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll be bringing our brand to life</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">You'll help define how we show up visually, creating designs that communicate our perspective and strengthen our identity.</p>
</div>
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll grow, develop and evolve</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">As part of a team that's always looking for new, innovative ways to offer value for our customers, you'll constantly be experimenting, learning, and trying out new things.</p>
</div>
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll take ownership</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">We expect you to take full responsibility and ownership of your tasks. Team leads avoid micromanaging and minimize interruptions so you can stay focused on your assignments.</p>
</div>
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll have experts at hand</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">Whenever you’re stuck, your teammates with a wide range of different expertise are ready to help you grow. And they’d love for you to share your knowledge too!</p>
</div>
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll pick where you work, every day</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">We embrace the remote culture. Every day you get to choose what environment makes you most productive.</p>
</div>
<div class="text-black dark:text-white/70">
<ul>
<li><strong><span class="h6 mb-1.5 block dark:text-white">You'll have stability</span></strong></li>
</ul>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">We value a stable workplace! MailerLite has been thriving for over 10 years and our year-over-year growth continues to increase.</p>
<p class="leading-relaxed opacity-60">&nbsp;</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>


<div class="container">
<div class="lg:px-20">
<div class="max-w-screen-lg">
<h2 class="mb-6">What you will work on</h2>
<div class="text text-xl leading-relaxed space-y-5">
<ul>
<li>
<p>Day-to-day design edits as well as larger creative projects</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Social media visuals, newsletter graphics, and website/blog imagery</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Feature pages and localized content for global audiences</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Broader marketing assets: e-books, presentations, covers, and banners</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Display advertising creatives across channels</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Design guidelines and brand consistency materials</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>


<div class="container">
<div class="lg:px-20">
<div class="max-w-screen-lg">
<h2 class="mb-6">&nbsp;</h2>
<h2 class="mb-6">What we expect from you</h2>
<div class="text text-xl leading-relaxed space-y-5">
<ul>
<li>
<p>3-5 years of experience as a graphic designer (portfolio required)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Strong working knowledge of color theory, typography, and grid layout</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Proficient in Figma and Canva; expert-level Adobe Photoshop skills</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Experience creating banners for display advertising and social media</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Comfortable working within brand guidelines and design systems</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Fluent written and verbal English communication skills</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>At least 4 hours overlap required with CET time zone</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>Bonus skills:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Copywriting skills to complement your visual work</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Experience with HTML and CSS for email or web design</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Ability to create animations, motion graphics, or short videos</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Familiarity with CSS frameworks (Tailwind, Bootstrap)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>A genuine affinity for brand identity and design systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>


<div class="container">
<div class="lg:px-20">
<div class="max-w-screen-lg">
<h2 class="mb-6">&nbsp;</h2>
<h2 class="mb-6">What we offer</h2>
<div class="text text-xl leading-relaxed space-y-5">
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Yearly gross salary range:</strong>&nbsp;€32,000 - €42,000</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Remote-first culture:&nbsp;</strong>Our team works remotely from around the world</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>International health insurance:&nbsp;</strong>Provided with coverage in most countries, with a monthly payout available in select countries where coverage is limited</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Company-paid retreats:&nbsp;</strong>Once a year, we gather for a week in a beautiful location to work, learn, and have fun together</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>31 days of vacation (including public holidays):&nbsp;</strong>We encourage you to unplug and recharge!</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>12 paid sick days:&nbsp;</strong>For your physical and mental well-being, no doctor's note required. Parents can use them to care for their sick children</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>4 creative days:&nbsp;</strong>One paid day off per quarter to do something creative and fun</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>12 parental days:&nbsp;</strong>Enjoy one paid day off each month to treasure time with your children</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Parental leave:&nbsp;</strong>100% paid leave when welcoming a new child through birth (3 months maternity, 1 month paternity) or adoption</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Parenting budget of $1000:&nbsp;</strong>A $1000 special gift to celebrate the arrival of your little one, whether through childbirth or adoption</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Joy Budget:&nbsp;</strong>Annual allowance to spend on what brings you joy, starting at $1,000 per year and increasing over time</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>MacBook and other tools:&nbsp;</strong>That help you to do your job efficiently</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>


<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mailerlite-graphic-designer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mailerlite-graphic-designer</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <media:content url="https://wwr-pro.s3.amazonaws.com/logos/0171/5387/logo.gif" type="image/png"/>
      <title>LawnStarter: Staff Software Engineer, Product</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>All Other Remote</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5387/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Porto Alegre, State of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://lawnstarter.com">http://lawnstarter.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>About LawnStarter</strong></p>
<p>LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services — operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.</p>
<p><strong>About Engineering at LawnStarter</strong></p>
<p>We build in small, focused initiative teams: a Product Engineer working alongside a PM and a designer, supported by an Engineering Manager who helps you grow. You'll also work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering peers across initiatives in a shared codebase. The whole team owns whether the work moves its metric.</p>
<p>AI coding agents are a force multiplier here — they give a small, senior team the leverage to ship more, faster, and at a higher bar for quality. We hire engineers who are wired for ownership and energized by shipping to a real marketplace with customers and pros on both sides.</p>
<p></p>
<p><strong>The Role</strong></p>
<p>You're the engineering anchor of an initiative — working as part of a tight team with your PM and designer, and alongside engineering peers on adjacent initiatives. You have a hand in the full lifecycle: shaping the problem, deciding the technical approach, directing AI agents to implement much of the code, shipping to production, and — with your team — owning the outcome.</p>
<p>You're measured by impact, not by lines of code merged. When an agent can ship something safely, your job is to make sure it's done right and the metric moves. When the work calls for careful, hand-written code in a sensitive area, you write it yourself.</p>
<p><strong>What makes this role exciting:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>You ship end-to-end.</strong> From problem-framing through production to the post-launch metric review — you see the whole arc and own the result with your team.</li>
<li>
<strong>You work as a true product partner.</strong> You sit at the table with PM and design, bringing engineering judgment to product calls and product sense to engineering calls.</li>
<li>
<strong>You get real autonomy — with the right checkpoints.</strong> You make most technical calls yourself, with architect review on significant architectural decisions and fast input from peers.</li>
<li>
<strong>You operate at a staff bar.</strong> You're trusted to make the call, ship the hard thing, and stand behind the outcome.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>What You'll Own</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>The technical approach</strong> — architecture, data model, integration choices, rollout plan, observability, and rollback strategy for your initiative. You make most calls yourself and bring significant architectural decisions to architect review; you document them, and revisit if the data says you were wrong.</li>
<li>
<strong>Implementation quality</strong> — the prompts, guardrails, evals, tests, and review loop that let agents ship safe, correct, production-ready code. Most lines will be agent-authored, and you're accountable for them — held to the same standard as the rest of the team working in a shared codebase.</li>
<li>
<strong>Cross-functional partnership</strong> — daily working contact with your PM (scope, tradeoffs) and designer (UX decisions, in-tool prototyping), regular collaboration with engineering peers, and weekly check-ins with your EM.</li>
<li>
<strong>The initiative outcome</strong> — the metric the initiative was set up to move. With your PM, you present results 2-4 weeks post-launch and share the "did it work" answer.</li>
<li>
<strong>A high bar for what ships</strong> — production correctness, security, performance, observability, and the experience for customers and pros. Agents accelerate you; they don't lower the bar.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Problems to Solve</h3>
<p></p>
<p><strong>Leading AI agents at a staff-level quality bar</strong> Most of the code on your initiative will be authored by AI agents. The craft is making them ship as if a senior engineer wrote it: prompts that encode our conventions, evals that catch issues before merge, tests that exercise the edges, observability that catches a regression before a customer does. How do you build a workflow that lets a small team ship far more than its size would suggest?</p>
<p><strong>Owning decisions with high autonomy</strong> You have real latitude to make and document technical calls quickly — with architect review on the big architectural ones and peers to pressure-test your thinking. How do you move fast, keep your team aligned, and stay accountable to the outcome?</p>
<p><strong>Shipping outcomes, not features</strong> Each initiative is measured by a metric — a conversion rate, a retention curve, a pro-funnel KPI, a unit-economics shift. You're accountable for the number alongside your team. How do you scope to actually move it, decide what *not* to build, and have the discipline to follow up 2-4 weeks after launch?</p>
<p></p>
<h3>What Success Looks Like (Year 1)</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>Initiative outcomes hit</strong> — You've shipped 3-4 initiatives end-to-end, and at least two clearly moved their metric (with the post-launch review to prove it).</li>
<li>
<strong>Agent workflow that travels</strong> — The prompts, evals, and review loop you built are picked up by peers on other initiatives.</li>
<li>
<strong>Faster cycle time</strong> — Median time from problem-framing to first production rollout on your initiatives is meaningfully shorter.</li>
<li>
<strong>Quality holds</strong> — No customer- or pro-facing regression traceable to agent-authored code that slipped through your review.</li>
<li>
<strong>Visible leverage</strong> — Peers point to artifacts you left behind — runbooks, evals, agent workflows, post-launch write-ups — as references they use.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Requirements</strong></p>
<h3>Who You Are</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI-native.</strong> Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or equivalent are how you ship today — daily, on production work. You have real opinions about prompts, evals, agent loops, and review workflows, and you know when to let the agent run versus write it yourself.</li>
<li>
<strong>Operating at a lead level.</strong> Whatever your current title, you've been the person making the call, shipping the hard thing, and standing behind whether it worked.</li>
<li>
<strong>Outcome-driven.</strong> You measure your week in "did the metric move" and "did the experience get better." You read the post-launch dashboard and own the answer.</li>
<li>
<strong>A strong horizontal partner.</strong> You hold your own with a strong PM and designer, and you collaborate well with engineering peers in a shared codebase. You bring engineering judgment to product calls and product judgment to engineering calls.</li>
<li>
<strong>Decisive and documented.</strong> You make architecture, data-model, and rollout calls, write them down, get fast input, and move.</li>
<li>
<strong>A force multiplier.</strong> Your impact compounds beyond your own initiative because you leave reusable artifacts behind — agent workflows, evals, runbooks, post-launch reviews.</li>
<li>
<strong>Customer- and pro-minded.</strong> This is a real marketplace with real people on both sides, and you care about the outcomes for both.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Good to Know</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>An individual-contributor role with room to grow.</strong> People management sits with the EM — but the path into management is an open door for those who want it.</li>
<li>
<strong>A product-engineering role, end-to-end.</strong> You ship features that move metrics; platform and architecture work happen inside the initiative when the outcome needs them.</li>
<li>
<strong>Hands-on, with a high quality bar.</strong> Agents handle much of the implementation; you bring the judgment, design, safety, and accountability. The bar is high.</li>
<li>
<strong>Shipping to a live marketplace.</strong> With $100M+ in bookings, customers and pros use what you ship within the same week.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Tech You'll Touch</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI agents</strong> — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, internal agent stack, MCP servers, evals tooling</li>
<li>
<strong>Backend</strong> — PHP/Laravel</li>
<li>
<strong>Frontend</strong> — TypeScript/React/React Native (customer &amp; pro apps, web and mobile)</li>
<li>
<strong>Data</strong> — Redshift, dbt, Segment, Airflow</li>
<li>
<strong>Infra</strong> — AWS, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub Actions</li>
<li>
<strong>Documentation &amp; process</strong> — Brain (Claude Code skills + docs repo), Confluence, Jira</li>
</ul>
<p>You don't need every box checked. You need deep skill in at least one of our stacks plus credible production experience with AI coding agents.</p>
<p><strong>Benefits</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Competitive salary of USD $80,000-$100,000 annual base</li>
<li>Work from anywhere</li>
<li>High ownership and autonomy</li>
<li>Fast-moving team that loves to build, learn, and grow</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product-4">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product-4</a></p>
</description>

      <expires_at>Wed, 01 Jul 2026 19:58:45 +0000</expires_at>
      <guid>https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product-4</guid>

    </item>

    <item>
      <title>LawnStarter: Staff Software Engineer, Product</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>All Other Remote</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Belo Horizonte, State of Minas Gerais, Brazil
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://lawnstarter.com">http://lawnstarter.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>About LawnStarter</strong></p>
<p>LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services — operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.</p>
<p><strong>About Engineering at LawnStarter</strong></p>
<p>We build in small, focused initiative teams: a Product Engineer working alongside a PM and a designer, supported by an Engineering Manager who helps you grow. You'll also work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering peers across initiatives in a shared codebase. The whole team owns whether the work moves its metric.</p>
<p>AI coding agents are a force multiplier here — they give a small, senior team the leverage to ship more, faster, and at a higher bar for quality. We hire engineers who are wired for ownership and energized by shipping to a real marketplace with customers and pros on both sides.</p>
<p></p>
<p><strong>The Role</strong></p>
<p>You're the engineering anchor of an initiative — working as part of a tight team with your PM and designer, and alongside engineering peers on adjacent initiatives. You have a hand in the full lifecycle: shaping the problem, deciding the technical approach, directing AI agents to implement much of the code, shipping to production, and — with your team — owning the outcome.</p>
<p>You're measured by impact, not by lines of code merged. When an agent can ship something safely, your job is to make sure it's done right and the metric moves. When the work calls for careful, hand-written code in a sensitive area, you write it yourself.</p>
<p><strong>What makes this role exciting:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>You ship end-to-end.</strong> From problem-framing through production to the post-launch metric review — you see the whole arc and own the result with your team.</li>
<li>
<strong>You work as a true product partner.</strong> You sit at the table with PM and design, bringing engineering judgment to product calls and product sense to engineering calls.</li>
<li>
<strong>You get real autonomy — with the right checkpoints.</strong> You make most technical calls yourself, with architect review on significant architectural decisions and fast input from peers.</li>
<li>
<strong>You operate at a staff bar.</strong> You're trusted to make the call, ship the hard thing, and stand behind the outcome.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>What You'll Own</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>The technical approach</strong> — architecture, data model, integration choices, rollout plan, observability, and rollback strategy for your initiative. You make most calls yourself and bring significant architectural decisions to architect review; you document them, and revisit if the data says you were wrong.</li>
<li>
<strong>Implementation quality</strong> — the prompts, guardrails, evals, tests, and review loop that let agents ship safe, correct, production-ready code. Most lines will be agent-authored, and you're accountable for them — held to the same standard as the rest of the team working in a shared codebase.</li>
<li>
<strong>Cross-functional partnership</strong> — daily working contact with your PM (scope, tradeoffs) and designer (UX decisions, in-tool prototyping), regular collaboration with engineering peers, and weekly check-ins with your EM.</li>
<li>
<strong>The initiative outcome</strong> — the metric the initiative was set up to move. With your PM, you present results 2-4 weeks post-launch and share the "did it work" answer.</li>
<li>
<strong>A high bar for what ships</strong> — production correctness, security, performance, observability, and the experience for customers and pros. Agents accelerate you; they don't lower the bar.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Problems to Solve</h3>
<p></p>
<p><strong>Leading AI agents at a staff-level quality bar</strong> Most of the code on your initiative will be authored by AI agents. The craft is making them ship as if a senior engineer wrote it: prompts that encode our conventions, evals that catch issues before merge, tests that exercise the edges, observability that catches a regression before a customer does. How do you build a workflow that lets a small team ship far more than its size would suggest?</p>
<p><strong>Owning decisions with high autonomy</strong> You have real latitude to make and document technical calls quickly — with architect review on the big architectural ones and peers to pressure-test your thinking. How do you move fast, keep your team aligned, and stay accountable to the outcome?</p>
<p><strong>Shipping outcomes, not features</strong> Each initiative is measured by a metric — a conversion rate, a retention curve, a pro-funnel KPI, a unit-economics shift. You're accountable for the number alongside your team. How do you scope to actually move it, decide what *not* to build, and have the discipline to follow up 2-4 weeks after launch?</p>
<p></p>
<h3>What Success Looks Like (Year 1)</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>Initiative outcomes hit</strong> — You've shipped 3-4 initiatives end-to-end, and at least two clearly moved their metric (with the post-launch review to prove it).</li>
<li>
<strong>Agent workflow that travels</strong> — The prompts, evals, and review loop you built are picked up by peers on other initiatives.</li>
<li>
<strong>Faster cycle time</strong> — Median time from problem-framing to first production rollout on your initiatives is meaningfully shorter.</li>
<li>
<strong>Quality holds</strong> — No customer- or pro-facing regression traceable to agent-authored code that slipped through your review.</li>
<li>
<strong>Visible leverage</strong> — Peers point to artifacts you left behind — runbooks, evals, agent workflows, post-launch write-ups — as references they use.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Requirements</strong></p>
<h3>Who You Are</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI-native.</strong> Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or equivalent are how you ship today — daily, on production work. You have real opinions about prompts, evals, agent loops, and review workflows, and you know when to let the agent run versus write it yourself.</li>
<li>
<strong>Operating at a lead level.</strong> Whatever your current title, you've been the person making the call, shipping the hard thing, and standing behind whether it worked.</li>
<li>
<strong>Outcome-driven.</strong> You measure your week in "did the metric move" and "did the experience get better." You read the post-launch dashboard and own the answer.</li>
<li>
<strong>A strong horizontal partner.</strong> You hold your own with a strong PM and designer, and you collaborate well with engineering peers in a shared codebase. You bring engineering judgment to product calls and product judgment to engineering calls.</li>
<li>
<strong>Decisive and documented.</strong> You make architecture, data-model, and rollout calls, write them down, get fast input, and move.</li>
<li>
<strong>A force multiplier.</strong> Your impact compounds beyond your own initiative because you leave reusable artifacts behind — agent workflows, evals, runbooks, post-launch reviews.</li>
<li>
<strong>Customer- and pro-minded.</strong> This is a real marketplace with real people on both sides, and you care about the outcomes for both.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Good to Know</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>An individual-contributor role with room to grow.</strong> People management sits with the EM — but the path into management is an open door for those who want it.</li>
<li>
<strong>A product-engineering role, end-to-end.</strong> You ship features that move metrics; platform and architecture work happen inside the initiative when the outcome needs them.</li>
<li>
<strong>Hands-on, with a high quality bar.</strong> Agents handle much of the implementation; you bring the judgment, design, safety, and accountability. The bar is high.</li>
<li>
<strong>Shipping to a live marketplace.</strong> With $100M+ in bookings, customers and pros use what you ship within the same week.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Tech You'll Touch</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI agents</strong> — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, internal agent stack, MCP servers, evals tooling</li>
<li>
<strong>Backend</strong> — PHP/Laravel</li>
<li>
<strong>Frontend</strong> — TypeScript/React/React Native (customer &amp; pro apps, web and mobile)</li>
<li>
<strong>Data</strong> — Redshift, dbt, Segment, Airflow</li>
<li>
<strong>Infra</strong> — AWS, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub Actions</li>
<li>
<strong>Documentation &amp; process</strong> — Brain (Claude Code skills + docs repo), Confluence, Jira</li>
</ul>
<p>You don't need every box checked. You need deep skill in at least one of our stacks plus credible production experience with AI coding agents.</p>
<p><strong>Benefits</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Competitive salary of USD $80,000-$100,000 annual base</li>
<li>Work from anywhere</li>
<li>High ownership and autonomy</li>
<li>Fast-moving team that loves to build, learn, and grow</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product-2">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product-2</a></p>

<h3>What Success Looks Like (Year 1)</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>Initiative outcomes hit</strong> — You've shipped 3-4 initiatives end-to-end, and at least two clearly moved their metric (with the post-launch review to prove it).</li>
<li>
<strong>Agent workflow that travels</strong> — The prompts, evals, and review loop you built are picked up by peers on other initiatives.</li>
<li>
<strong>Faster cycle time</strong> — Median time from problem-framing to first production rollout on your initiatives is meaningfully shorter.</li>
<li>
<strong>Quality holds</strong> — No customer- or pro-facing regression traceable to agent-authored code that slipped through your review.</li>
<li>
<strong>Visible leverage</strong> — Peers point to artifacts you left behind — runbooks, evals, agent workflows, post-launch write-ups — as references they use.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Requirements</strong></p>
<h3>Who You Are</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI-native.</strong> Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or equivalent are how you ship today — daily, on production work. You have real opinions about prompts, evals, agent loops, and review workflows, and you know when to let the agent run versus write it yourself.</li>
<li>
<strong>Operating at a lead level.</strong> Whatever your current title, you've been the person making the call, shipping the hard thing, and standing behind whether it worked.</li>
<li>
<strong>Outcome-driven.</strong> You measure your week in "did the metric move" and "did the experience get better." You read the post-launch dashboard and own the answer.</li>
<li>
<strong>A strong horizontal partner.</strong> You hold your own with a strong PM and designer, and you collaborate well with engineering peers in a shared codebase. You bring engineering judgment to product calls and product judgment to engineering calls.</li>
<li>
<strong>Decisive and documented.</strong> You make architecture, data-model, and rollout calls, write them down, get fast input, and move.</li>
<li>
<strong>A force multiplier.</strong> Your impact compounds beyond your own initiative because you leave reusable artifacts behind — agent workflows, evals, runbooks, post-launch reviews.</li>
<li>
<strong>Customer- and pro-minded.</strong> This is a real marketplace with real people on both sides, and you care about the outcomes for both.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Good to Know</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>An individual-contributor role with room to grow.</strong> People management sits with the EM — but the path into management is an open door for those who want it.</li>
<li>
<strong>A product-engineering role, end-to-end.</strong> You ship features that move metrics; platform and architecture work happen inside the initiative when the outcome needs them.</li>
<li>
<strong>Hands-on, with a high quality bar.</strong> Agents handle much of the implementation; you bring the judgment, design, safety, and accountability. The bar is high.</li>
<li>
<strong>Shipping to a live marketplace.</strong> With $100M+ in bookings, customers and pros use what you ship within the same week.</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<h3>Tech You'll Touch</h3>
<p></p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>AI agents</strong> — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, internal agent stack, MCP servers, evals tooling</li>
<li>
<strong>Backend</strong> — PHP/Laravel</li>
<li>
<strong>Frontend</strong> — TypeScript/React/React Native (customer &amp; pro apps, web and mobile)</li>
<li>
<strong>Data</strong> — Redshift, dbt, Segment, Airflow</li>
<li>
<strong>Infra</strong> — AWS, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub Actions</li>
<li>
<strong>Documentation &amp; process</strong> — Brain (Claude Code skills + docs repo), Confluence, Jira</li>
</ul>
<p>You don't need every box checked. You need deep skill in at least one of our stacks plus credible production experience with AI coding agents.</p>
<p><strong>Benefits</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Competitive salary of USD $80,000-$100,000 annual base</li>
<li>Work from anywhere</li>
<li>High ownership and autonomy</li>
<li>Fast-moving team that loves to build, learn, and grow</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lawnstarter-staff-software-engineer-product</a></p>
</description>

      <media:content url="https://wwr-pro.s3.amazonaws.com/logos/0171/5382/logo.gif" type="image/png"/>
      <title>Bask Health: Senior Customer Success Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Customer Support</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5382/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> new york, New York, United States
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://bask.health">http://bask.health</a>
</p>

<p>Bask is the best telehealth platform on the market. Our mission is to empower entrepreneurs and businesses to launch a DTC telemedicine company quickly and easily, abstracting away the complexities of the industry into an easy-to-use platform. With hundreds of features to meet the unique needs of telehealth, we put all the tools in our customers' hands for success. We are a rapidly growing startup, and we work with care and intention to create a high-performance company with the following in mind:</p>
<ul>
<li>Speed Wins. Make decisions, move quickly, and know that if things go wrong, it's okay for you and the company.</li>
<li>Intuition, Then Data. We're a data-driven company. We start with our instincts and then use data to validate our decisions and improve</li>
<li>Miles, Not Inches. Thinking small is a self-fulfilling prophecy. Favor bold ideas over incremental changes</li>
<li>Customer-Obsession. We are obsessed with helping all our customers launch multi-billion-dollar companies with ease. We accomplish this by knowing our customers incredibly well and finding ways to make their businesses better.</li>
<li>Ownership &amp; Accountability, together. We embody a culture of extreme ownership, accountability, and teamwork. We count on every team member to take responsibility for their work, embrace a proactive mindset to overcome challenges, and work together to achieve our collective success</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<p>A look into the day to day:</p>
<ul>
<li>Building logic based questionnaires (asynchronous visits) through the Bask software (if you have experience with building logic based workflows, you'll be great at this!)</li>
<li>Helping customers launch and upsell medications from start to to finish</li>
<li>Triaging bask software and order related questions and issues that come up for any of your accounts, any time of the day</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<p></p>
<p>We're Looking For</p>
<p>A self-starter who is passionate about enhancing the customer experience; you take pride in demonstrating the value of the products and services to your customers. You think outside of the box, excel at creative problem-solving, and are comfortable taking on projects that you have potentially never done before! You are a people-person - empathetic, self-aware, low ego, and extremely positive - and are comfortable working in a small collaborative team where you'll wear many hats. You are passionate and proficient in written and spoken communications.</p>
<p></p>
<p>Customer Success at Bask Health is AI-first and customer-obsessed. Work starts in an LLM to clarify intent and context, moves into research and execution, is validated with real patients and partners, and is continuously refined as we learn. AI and self-serve research are default parts of the workflow, not side experiments.</p>
<p>We are looking for Customer Success people who take full ownership of their accounts, treat AI as a real collaborator, and care deeply about the patient and provider experience.</p>
<p>What You'll Do</p>
<ul>
<li>Work AI-first: Use LLMs as your starting point to draft communications, structure problems, and think through customer needs. Apply your own judgment to refine the output and make it human.</li>
<li>Validate with real customers and iterate: Test your approaches with real patients, providers, and partners. Use what you learn to improve how we serve them — before issues become patterns.</li>
<li>Make automation legible and trustworthy: Help customers understand what Bask's platform is doing on their behalf. Communicate clearly and proactively to build trust while keeping their experience simple.</li>
</ul>
<p>Share AI-native workflows: Document prompts, processes, and workflows that work. Share them across the Customer Success team so we raise the bar together.</p>
<p></p>
<p></p>
<p>In this job, you will:</p>
<ul>
<li>Build strong relationships with Bask's customers and own the full customer lifecycle, including onboarding, value realization, engagement strategies, expansion, and renewals</li>
<li>Partner with customer stakeholders to develop custom engagement initiatives that drive user adoption and support the unique needs of their patients</li>
<li>Manage all current customer data</li>
<li>Analyze customer engagement metrics and use them to communicate value, trends, and opportunities with key stakeholders</li>
<li>Deliver program demos, provide insightful technical answers, and recommend creative ways to get the most out of the Bask platform</li>
<li>Finding comfort in working in a fast-paced startup environment</li>
<li>Believing no task is too small and no task is too tall</li>
</ul>
<p></p>
<p><strong>Requirements</strong></p>
<ul> <li>7+ years of experience in Customer Success, Client Services, or Customer Success at a SaaS organization</li> <li>Experience in e-commerce</li> <li>Experience with large- to enterprise-sized customer book of business</li> <li>Creative problem solver with a determination to succeed</li> <li>Independent and motivated, with the wisdom to seek help where needed</li> <li>An entrepreneur</li> <li>Highly articulate, ability to communicate effectively both when speaking and writing</li> <li>Highly organized, with the ability to juggle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment</li> <li>Comfortable collaborating with different teams (product, sales, marketing, etc.)</li> </ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/bask-health-senior-customer-success-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/bask-health-senior-customer-success-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <media:content url="https://wwr-pro.s3.amazonaws.com/logos/0169/0644/logo.gif" type="image/png"/>
      <title>Lightup Network Solutions GmbH & Co. KG: Technical Product Lead for Innovative Telecom Services - 100% Remote - 6 hours per day</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>Hessen</state>
      <skills>Linux, DevOps, and Product Designer</skills>
      <category>DevOps and Sysadmin</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0169/0644/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Frankfurt, Germany
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.lightupnet.de">https://www.lightupnet.de</a>
</p>

<h1>Technical Product Lead for Innovative Telecom Services - 100% Remote - 6 hours per day</h1>
<h2>Creating Telecom-centric Web Services</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<p>We are a small, 100% remote telecommunications services company based in Germany and Switzerland established in 1998. Our team currently consists of 10 people working remotely across different countries and time zones.</p>
<p>We build and operate innovative web-based products in the telecommunications and ISP space for <strong>retail, business and enterprise clients and telecom operators</strong>. Our projects sit at the intersection of telecom infrastructure, customer-facing web applications, automation, billing, provisioning, and operational processes.</p>
<p>We are now looking for a <strong>Technical Product Lead&nbsp;</strong>to take ownership of translating complex telecom product ideas into clear technical concepts, development tasks, and working software.</p>
<h2>The role</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<p>We are looking for someone with a strong technical background who can understand complex infrastructure-driven services, structure them into practical web projects, and lead developers through implementation.</p>
<p>You will work directly with the <strong>G</strong><strong>eneral Manager</strong> and act as the bridge between business ideas, telecom/ISP knowledge, product requirements, and software development.</p>
<p>Your job is to make sure that ideas become clear specifications, that developers know exactly what to build, and that projects move forward with quality, speed, and technical sanity.</p>
<h2>Responsibilities</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<ul>
<li>Translate high-level product and business ideas into clear technical concepts, specifications, user stories, and development tickets</li>
<li>Lead and coordinate a small remote developer team</li>
<li>Break down complex telecom/ISP workflows into practical web application features</li>
<li>Define MVPs, priorities, milestones, and delivery plans</li>
<li>Review developer output from a functional and technical perspective</li>
<li>Challenge unclear requirements, weak architecture, and poor implementation choices</li>
<li>Ensure that web applications are built with scalability, maintainability, security, and operational reality in mind</li>
<li>Help design customer portals, admin backends, provisioning flows, billing-related workflows, APIs, dashboards, and internal tools</li>
<li>Identify technical risks, blockers, dependencies, and missing pieces early</li>
<li>Keep projects moving without unnecessary meetings or corporate overhead</li>
<li>Report progress, risks, and decisions directly to the General Manager</li>
</ul>
<h2>What we are looking for</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<p>You should be a technical generalist with a strong product sense.</p>
<p>Ideally, you come from one or more of these backgrounds:</p>
<ul>
<li>Technical Product Lead</li>
<li>Engineering Lead</li>
<li>Tech Lead</li>
<li>Solution Architect</li>
<li>Senior DevOps Engineer</li>
<li>Senior Sysadmin</li>
<li>ISP / Telecom Engineer</li>
<li>Technical company builder</li>
<li>Fractional CTO</li>
</ul>
<p>You do not necessarily need to be the main developer yourself, but you must understand enough to lead developers, evaluate technical decisions, and know when something is overbuilt, underbuilt, unclear, or simply wrong.</p>
<h2>Required experience</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<ul>
<li>Strong understanding of web applications, APIs, databases, backend systems, and software delivery</li>
<li>Profound knowledge of Linux (CentOS, Ubuntu, other distros) environments</li>
<li>Good understanding of networking concepts such as TCP/IP, DNS, routing, IPv4/IPv6, firewalls, VPNs and related infrastructure</li>
<li>Experience working with developers and turning requirements into clear technical tasks</li>
<li>Ability to structure complex systems into manageable modules, milestones, and deliverables</li>
<li>Practical understanding of security, monitoring, logging, reliability, and operational requirements</li>
<li>Experience working in remote teams</li>
<li>Strong written communication skills in English</li>
<li>Perfect English grammar without using AI</li>
<li>Ability to work independently and take ownership</li>
</ul>
<h2>Nice to have</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR. Yes, sorry for repeating this over and over and but people tend to just scroll and apply without reading, so we want to make sure this is clear from the beginning. :-)</p>
<ul>
<li>Experience in telecommunications, ISP, hosting, datacenter, VoIP, SIP, cloud infrastructure, or B2B SaaS</li>
<li>Understanding of Linux environments, many years of experience with Linux/GNU command line tools from a time before AI, Python, Bash and/or PHP or all of them, other programming/scripting languages, Proxmox, Virtual Machines, Apache/nginx, MariaDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, server hardware &amp; networking gear, IPMI / KVM-over-IP, BGP, IP address management, CIDR, provisioning systems, billing workflows, customer portals, network automation</li>
<li>Experience building or managing self-service platforms for customers or internal teams</li>
<li>Experience with small companies, startups, founder-led teams, or distributed freelancer teams</li>
<li>Hands-on development experience, even if you no longer want to code full-time</li>
<li>German language skills are a plus, but not mandatory</li>
</ul>
<h2>What makes this role different</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<p>This role is for someone who enjoys the messy middle between product, technology, business, and execution.</p>
<p>You should be comfortable taking a rough idea like:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>“We need a customer portal for managing telecom services, provisioning, billing, and support workflows.”</p>
</blockquote>
<p>and turning it into:</p>
<ul>
<li>a clear product concept</li>
<li>a technical architecture</li>
<li>implementation phases</li>
<li>developer tickets</li>
<li>acceptance criteria</li>
<li>priorities</li>
<li>risks</li>
<li>delivery checkpoints</li>
</ul>
<p>We need someone who understands what should be built, why it matters, and how to get developers to build it properly.</p>
<h2>Working style</h2>
<p>You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<ul>
<li>100% Remote</li>
<li>Freelancer agreement</li>
<li><strong>6 hours</strong> per day work-time (Monday to Friday)</li>
<li>Monthly salary: <strong>2000 EUR</strong></li>
<li>Direct collaboration with the General Manager</li>
<li>Small team, fast decisions, low bureaucracy</li>
<li>Focus on practical delivery, not corporate process</li>
</ul>
<h2>You may be a good fit if you...</h2>
<p>By the way, you would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<ul>
<li>Have a strong technical background but also understand product and customers</li>
<li>Can speak with developers, management, and business stakeholders</li>
<li>Are comfortable with telecom, ISP, infrastructure, and web platforms</li>
<li>Like creating clarity from vague ideas</li>
<li>Can write excellent technical specifications and tickets</li>
<li>Know how to push developers without micromanaging them</li>
<li>Prefer pragmatic execution over endless meetings</li>
<li>Have worked in small, remote, technically complex environments</li>
<li>Can challenge assumptions and make things move forward</li>
</ul>
<h2>How to apply</h2>
<p>PS: You would work 6 hours per day, Monday to Friday. Freelancer contract. The monthly salary is 2000 EUR.</p>
<p>You can apply via WWR or by sending an E-Mail to <a href="mailto:jobs@lightupnet.de">jobs@lightupnet.de</a></p>
<p>Please send us:</p>
<ul>
<li>A short introduction about your background</li>
<li>Ideally, your CV</li>
<li>Relevant projects you have led or helped build</li>
<li>Your experience with telecom, ISP, infrastructure, SaaS, or web platforms</li>
</ul>
<p>You can apply via WWR or by sending an E-Mail to <a href="mailto:jobs@lightupnet.de">jobs@lightupnet.de</a></p>
<p>We are especially interested in people who have previously worked as technical company builders, engineering leads, solution architects, senior sysadmins, DevOps leads, or technical product leads in infrastructure-heavy environments.</p>
<p>You can apply via WWR or by sending an E-Mail to <a href="mailto:jobs@lightupnet.de">jobs@lightupnet.de</a></p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lightup-network-solutions-gmbh-co-kg-technical-product-lead-for-innovative-telecom-services">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lightup-network-solutions-gmbh-co-kg-technical-product-lead-for-innovative-telecom-services</a></p>
</description>

      <guid>https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lightup-network-solutions-gmbh-co-kg-technical-product-lead-for-innovative-telecom-services</guid>

      <state>Alabama</state>

      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5379/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Infiniti Group Ltd
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://infinitiownerclub.com">https://infinitiownerclub.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>Application link</strong>:&nbsp;<a title="https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely" href="https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely">https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely</a><br><br></p>
<div class="vacancy-intro reveal reveal-delay-2 is-visible">
<p>Infiniti Group operates at the crossroads of digital assets, analytical market research, and algorithm-informed trading methods. We are dedicated to building a team of motivated beginners who want hands-on exposure to financial markets and real trading situations.</p>
<p>We are looking for a&nbsp;<strong>Junior Crypto Trader</strong>&nbsp;— a role designed for those at the very start of their professional journey. If you're interested in reading market trends, executing trades, and working with data to inform your decisions, this is a great fit.</p>
<p>You will trade independently but with structured oversight and regular feedback from experienced traders. There are no strict performance targets. Our focus is on developing your practical trading abilities in live crypto markets. No previous work experience is needed — we provide complete training.</p>
</div>
<div class="vacancy-block reveal reveal-delay-3 is-visible">
<h3>Key Responsibilities</h3>
<ul>
<li>Place buy and sell orders in cryptocurrency markets while following risk rules and basic strategies.</li>
<li>Watch real-time price changes, trading volumes, and order book movements.</li>
<li>Use charts, technical indicators, and market signals to guide trade entries and exits.</li>
<li>Keep track of active positions and assess potential gains or losses.</li>
<li>Follow crypto news and understand how events may affect prices.</li>
<li>Work with industry-standard trading interfaces and analytics dashboards.</li>
<li>Review your own trading results regularly to identify strengths and areas to improve.</li>
<li>Build a deeper understanding of market behavior and trading techniques over time.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div class="vacancy-block reveal reveal-delay-4 is-visible">
<h3>What We Offer</h3>
<ul>
<li>A chance to grow inside an international company with a global outlook.</li>
<li>100% remote work — you can operate from any location.</li>
<li>Flexible hours and adjustable workload.</li>
<li>Access to professional-grade trading tools and live market data.</li>
<li>Use of advanced analytics and reporting systems.</li>
<li>A clear roadmap for professional growth with increasing responsibility over time.</li>
<li>Ongoing help and feedback from seasoned market practitioners.</li>
<li>Hands-on training on professional trading platforms, guided by experienced mentors.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div class="vacancy-block reveal reveal-delay-5 is-visible">
<h3>Requirements</h3>
<ul>
<li>Basic computer literacy (using browsers, platforms, hotkeys).</li>
<li>Stable internet connection and a laptop or PC.</li>
<li>English at intermediate level or higher (to understand news and interface).</li>
<li>Ability to focus during market hours (flexible, but attentive).</li>
<li>Willingness to learn and ask questions.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Application link</strong>:&nbsp;<a title="https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely" href="https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely">https://infinitiownerclub.com/work/weworkremotely</a></p>
</div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/infiniti-group-ltd-entry-level-junior-trader-remote-1">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/infiniti-group-ltd-entry-level-junior-trader-remote-1</a></p>

      <description>

<p>

</p>

<div class="single-job-form">
<p>At <strong>Linkage Web Solutions</strong>, we’re growing our network of high-performing Virtual Assistants—and right now, we’re building a <strong>priority pool of Lead Generation Specialists</strong> for upcoming client opportunities.</p>
<p>If you’re skilled at finding, qualifying, and reaching out to leads—this is your chance to get matched with international clients.</p>
<p><strong>Why Join Our Talent Pool?</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Priority access</strong> to client openings</li>
<li><strong>Fast-tracked hiring</strong> once matched</li>
<li>Optional <strong>Free GoHighLevel (GHL) Training</strong> to upgrade your skills</li>
<li><strong>$4–$5/hour</strong> competitive rate</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>What You’ll Do (Depending on Client Needs)</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Research and identify potential leads (B2B or B2C)</li>
<li>Build and manage lead lists using tools or CRMs</li>
<li>Conduct outreach via email, LinkedIn, or other platforms</li>
<li>Qualify leads based on client criteria</li>
<li>Maintain accurate records and pipeline tracking</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience in <strong>lead generation, prospecting, or outreach</strong></li>
<li>Familiarity with tools like LinkedIn, Apollo, CRM systems, or similar</li>
<li>Strong written English (especially for outreach messages)</li>
<li>Detail-oriented and results-driven</li>
<li>Able to work independently in a remote setup</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience in cold email or LinkedIn outreach campaigns</li>
<li>Knowledge of GoHighLevel (GHL) or similar platforms</li>
<li>Basic understanding of sales funnels or appointment setting</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Important Note</strong></p>
<p>This is part of our <strong>active candidate pooling</strong>. There is no fixed start date, but <strong>qualified candidates are prioritized and contacted first</strong> once a matching opportunity becomes available.</p>
<p>Send your resume to <strong><a class="__cf_email__" href="/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection">[email&nbsp;protected]</a></strong><br>
<strong>Subject:</strong> <em>Lead Generation Specialist – Active Pooling</em></p>

design.</p><br><br><strong>Benefits</strong><br><p>Compensation: Competitive based on experience</p>

      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5358/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>

    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://swansonvitamins.com">http://swansonvitamins.com</a>
</p>

<p><span><span>Competitive Benefits • Fitness Center Reimbursement • Product Discounts</span></span><span><br></span><br><br></p>
<p><span><span>Who We Are:</span></span></p>
<p><span>Swanson Health Products has been committed to bringing wellness to the world for over 50 years. As a leader in the vitamins, supplements, and natural wellness industry, we focus on delivering science-backed, high-quality products that support healthier lives. We are a digitally driven organization investing in innovation, customer experience, and data-informed growth.</span><span>&nbsp;</span></p>
<p><span>&nbsp;</span></p>
<p><span><span>About the Role:</span></span></p>
<p>We are seeking a <span>Shopify Developer</span> to build, optimize, and scale our Shopify Plus storefront. This role is critical in delivering high-performance eCommerce experiences, supporting subscription models, and enabling rapid experimentation across marketing, merchandising, and IT.</p>
<p><br></p>
<p>You’ll work across front-end development, back-end integrations, and performance optimization within a complex, enterprise-level Shopify ecosystem.</p>
<p><br></p>
<p>As an organization, we are new to Shopify and recently launched the platform for Swanson Health Products, making this an exciting opportunity to help shape and evolve our eCommerce foundation from the ground up.</p>
<p><br></p>
<p><span><span>What You'll Do:</span></span><span><br></span></p>
<p><span><span>Shopify Development &amp; Customization</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Develop and maintain custom Shopify themes using Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript</li>
<li>Build reusable components to support high-SKU catalog navigation and merchandising</li>
<li>Customize Shopify Plus features, including checkout extensibility, scripts, and functions</li>
</ul>
<p><span><span>Integrations &amp; Systems Architecture</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Integrate Shopify with key business systems, including:
<ul>
<li>ERP (Mozart), CMS, and subscription platforms (e.g., Ordergroove)</li>
<li>Search and personalization tools (e.g., Constructor)</li>
<li>Marketing platforms (e.g., Cordial, reviews, loyalty programs)</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Work with APIs to ensure seamless data flow across systems</li>
</ul>
<p><span><span>Performance &amp; Optimization</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Optimize site speed, Core Web Vitals, and overall conversion performance</li>
<li>Implement A/B testing and personalization strategies</li>
<li>Improve checkout performance and mobile user experience</li>
</ul>
<p><span><span>Subscription &amp; Customer Experience</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Enhance subscription journeys and customer account management experiences</li>
<li>Support personalized product recommendations and promotional strategies</li>
<li>Build scalable features that drive retention and conversion growth</li>
</ul>
<p><span><span>Maintenance &amp; Troubleshooting</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Debug and resolve production issues in a timely manner</li>
<li>Ensure site uptime, reliability, and scalability</li>
<li>Continuously improve site architecture, performance, and code quality</li>
</ul>
<p><br></p>
<p><span><span>Required Qualifications:</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>3+ years of experience working with Shopify or Shopify Plus</li>
<li>Strong proficiency in:
<ul>
<li>Liquid, JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5, and CSS3</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Hands-on experience with:
<ul>
<li>Shopify APIs, webhooks, and app integrations</li>
<li>Version control systems (e.g., GitHub)</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Solid understanding of eCommerce fundamentals, including:
<ul>
<li>Checkout flows, subscriptions, merchandising, and SEO</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Experience integrating third-party services and APIs</li>
</ul>
<p><br></p>
<p><span><span>Preferred Qualifications:</span></span></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience with subscription-based commerce platforms (e.g., Ordergroove or similar)</li>
<li>Familiarity with headless commerce architectures, including Shopify Hydrogen</li>
<li>Experience with personalization and search platforms (e.g., Constructor)</li>
<li>Knowledge of analytics and experimentation tools, including GA4, Amplitude, and A/B testing platforms</li>
<li>Exposure to enterprise-level eCommerce environments, including large product catalogs and global scale operations</li>
</ul>

<p>Quer trabalhar em uma empresa que investe na sua carreira e, ainda, valoriza a educação e a tecnologia?</p>
<p>Então #VemPraAlura #MergulheEmTecnologia</p>
<p>É importante dizer que valorizamos a diversidade e estamos buscando tornar o nosso ambiente mais plural e inclusivo, por isso, incentivamos que mulheres, pessoas negras e indígenas e pessoas LGBTQIA+ se inscrevam.</p>
<p>Se fez sentido para você, esperamos sua inscrição!</p>
<p>Em sua atuação como....</p>
<p><strong>Responsabilidades e atribuições</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Realizar prospecção ativa via telefone, e-mail, LinkedIn e WhatsApp.</li>
<li>Identificar e mapear leads qualificados dentro do perfil ideal de cliente (ICP).</li>
<li>Utilizar técnicas de vendas consultivas para engajar potenciais clientes e entender suas&nbsp;necessidades.</li>
<li>Alimentar e atualizar o CRM (HubSpot) com informações precisas e estratégicas.</li>
<li>Criar cadências de contato personalizadas e realizar follow-ups de qualidade.</li>
<li>Colaborar com o time de Growth e Marketing para otimizar estratégias de prospecção.</li>
<li>Gerar SQLs (Sales Qualified Leads) para o time de vendas, garantindo um pipeline consistente e&nbsp;qualificado.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Requisitos e qualificações</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experiência prévia como BDR, SDR ou em funções comerciais relacionadas (mínimo de 6&nbsp;meses).</li>
<li>Conhecimento em técnicas de prospecção e vendas consultivas (SPIN, BANT, etc.).</li>
<li>Experiência no uso de CRMs (HubSpot é um diferencial).</li>
<li>Habilidade para trabalhar com ferramentas de prospecção, como LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Ramper ou similares.</li>
<li>Familiaridade com KPIs de prospecção (número de leads trabalhados, taxa de conversão, etc.).XXXX</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Não é obrigatório, mas seria legal se você tivesse:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Certificações ou cursos na área de vendas (Inbound Sales, Outbound Sales, etc.).</li>
<li>Experiência com prospecção em empresas do segmento de tecnologia ou educação.</li>
<li>Habilidade para personalizar cadências de prospecção e abordagens estratégicas.</li>
<li>Vivência em um ambiente comercial 100% remoto.</li>
<li>Conhecimento em marketing digital e estratégias de Growth.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Informações adicionais</strong><br><strong>Salário, saúde, alimentação e bem estar:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Vale-Refeição: R$710,00 por mês (depositado em cartão de benefício flexível);</li>
<li>Vale-Alimentação: R$167,00 por mês;</li>
<li>Apoio home-office, que você poderá usar como preferir: R$90,00 por mês;</li>
<li>Convênio médico (cobertura de 70%) e odontológico (opcional) da SulAmérica Saúde;</li>
<li>Licenças maternidade e paternidade estendidas;</li>
<li>Seguro de vida;&nbsp;</li>
<li>Gympass;</li>
<li>Day off no mês do seu aniversário;</li>
<li>Suporte de equipamentos para o seu local de trabalho ser o mais confortável possível.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Ambiente de trabalho:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Horários de trabalho flexíveis;</li>
<li>Dress code criado por você;</li>
<li>Cultura de trabalho humanizada que respeita e valoriza todas as pessoas, incentivando a diversidade e a inclusão;</li>
<li>Meio colaborativo e com abertura para propor ideias e ajudar na construção de projetos e ações;</li>
<li>Eventos internos que abordam temáticas de grande relevância para a sociedade, favorecendo a disseminação do conhecimento.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Desenvolvimento profissional e pessoal:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>One on One com a sua liderança, um momento para falar sobre sua carreira e/ou sua vida pessoal. A ideia é que seja um espaço aberto e acolhedor para você levar o que quiser;</li>
<li>Ambiente horizontal, ou seja, não contamos com uma progressão de carreira em escadinha. No lugar disso, queremos que você foque no seu desenvolvimento pessoal e daremos todo o incentivo para que você possa crescer e ter oportunidades em suas áreas de interesse;</li>
<li>Networking com grandes profissionais da área da educação e da tecnologia;</li>
<li>Acesso gratuito às plataformas de educação e tecnologia do Grupo Alura (Alura, Alura Língua, PM3 e e-books da Casa do Código) para que você se desenvolva em todos os âmbitos que deseja e explore novos horizontes.</li>
<li>Desconto em graduação, pós-graduação e MBA na FIAP;</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>E aí, você quer colaborar com a gente para transformar vidas e carreiras? Se inscreva em nossa vaga!</strong></p>
<p><strong>MAIS SOBRE NÓS:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Artigo sobre nossa cultura e valores</strong>: <a href="https://www.alura.com.br/artigos/grupo-alura">https://www.alura.com.br/artigos/grupo-alura</a></li>
<li><strong>Artigo sobre nosso ambiente de trabalho</strong>:&nbsp;<a href="https://www.alura.com.br/artigos/5-curiosidades-como-e-trabalhar-grupo-alura">https://www.alura.com.br/artigos/5-curiosidades-como-e-trabalhar-grupo-alura</a></li>
</ul>
<p>&nbsp;</p></div>

      <title>Stone & Tile: Administrative Assistant</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country>🇺🇸 United States of America</country>
      <state>Illinois</state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Design</category>
      <type>Contract</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Chicago
</p>

<p>Administrative Assistant<br><br>Remote | Stone &amp; Tile Industry<br><br>Our growing stone and tile company is seeking a professional, organized, and detail-oriented Administrative Assistant to support daily operations, estimating coordination, and customer communications. This remote position plays an important role in assisting our team with vendor coordination, proposal preparation, scheduling updates, and project follow-up.<br><br>We are looking for a motivated individual who can work independently, communicate professionally, and thrive in a fast-paced construction-related environment.<br><br></p>
<div>Key Responsibilities<br><br>* Communicate with stone and tile vendors, manufacturers, and suppliers to obtain material pricing for project estimates and quotations<br>* Prepare professional proposals, transmittals, cover sheets, warranty letters, and related customer documentation<br>* Maintain and update project schedules and tracking reports using Microsoft Excel, Word, and other office software<br>* Send follow-up correspondence regarding previously quoted projects and ongoing customer inquiries<br>* Manage and organize company email communications in a professional and timely manner<br>* Provide administrative support to estimating, sales, and project management teams as needed<br>* Maintain accurate digital records and assist with document organization<br><br></div>
<div>&nbsp;Qualifications<br><br>* Previous administrative assistant or office coordination experience preferred<br>* Strong written and verbal communication skills<br>* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and Word<br>* Ability to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and manage multiple assignments simultaneously<br>* Self-motivated with the ability to work independently in a remote environment<br>* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail<br>* Experience in the construction, stone, tile, or building materials industry is a plus<br><br>&nbsp;What We Offer<br><br>* Remote work opportunity<br>* Company email account provided<br>* Weekly compensation<br>* Professional growth opportunities within a growing company<br>* Supportive and team-oriented work environment<br><br>Position Type<br><br>* Full-Time or Part-Time<br>* Remote<br><br>We are seeking a dependable professional who values organization, communication, and customer service excellence while contributing to the continued growth of our company.</div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stone-tile-administrative-assistant">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stone-tile-administrative-assistant</a></p>
</description>

      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5346/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> ignitionit.com
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://ignitionit.com">https://ignitionit.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>Fully Remote · Work from home · Full-time (Eastern or Pacific time zones) · Apple-first&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p><strong>TL;DR</strong></p>
<p>ignitionit.com is a seasoned, Apple-first MSP — in business for almost 30 years — and we're hiring a Help Desk Technician to be the friendly, capable first voice our clients reach when something isn't working. This position is a remote, work-from-home, full-time role.</p>
<p>We've written this as a junior-to-mid-level job description because that's where most great people start on our team. But if you're more seasoned — comfortable in identity, MDM, or security work — don't scroll past. We'd be delighted to find someone who can step in higher up the ladder, and we'll happily talk about a more senior scope and comp if that's you.</p>
<p><strong>What the actual job???</strong></p>
<p>Unlike many IT shops, we believe that the technology is the tactic. The actual job is making people feel taken care of. One of our core values is “49% tech support, 51% human support.”</p>
<p>You'll succeed by configuring devices, resetting access, untangling email, and chasing down the gremlin that's been haunting someone's conference room. But you'll know you succeeded when a client exhales and says, “Thank goodness you're on it!” That Warm Fuzzy Feeling™ is the actual product. The green lights on the dashboard are just how we get there.</p>
<p>In a typical week, you might:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Onboard a new hire at a fast-growing startup: provision their Mac, set up their Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 accounts, enroll the device in MDM, and make sure day one feels effortless.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Offboard a departing employee cleanly — recover the laptop, revoke access, and close the loop so nothing's left dangling. All boxes checked, ticket notated and closed, client followed up with.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Field a "my email stopped working" ticket that turns out to be a DNS thing, a phishing-filter thing, or a "they changed their password on their phone three weeks ago" thing.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Help a family office or RIA get a touchy Zoom Room or conference-room AV setup behaving before a meeting — over the phone.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Triage the queue so phones get answered fast, emails don't rot, and nobody feels forgotten — even when the honest answer is, “We're on it, but here's the realistic timeline."</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>⠀ You'll be the front line: helping with what you can, researching what you can't, and pinging the rest of the team when you're genuinely stuck. That last part is a skill, not a weakness — knowing when to ask is half the job.</p>
<p><strong>About ignitionit.com</strong></p>
<p>We're one of the oldest Apple Technical Partners on the planet, and one of the only certified Apple Premier Partners — that’s kinda like getting knighted by the ghost of Steve Jobs. We started where you’d expect to find Macs (creative agencies, non-profits, education) but our primary clients today are cutting-edge, venture-backed AI SaaS startups, VC and private equity firms, registered investment advisors — many of them operating under stringent compliance expectations (SOC 2, SEC). That means we hold ourselves to a higher standard than the scrappy shop we started as. Polished, reliable, security-minded work is the baseline now — but always with a smile!</p>
<p>What hasn't changed: relationships are still our most valuable currency — with clients, with vendors, and with each other. We're a tight, supportive team. We teach each other what we know, cover for each other when life throws a punch, and care a little too much about doing things the right way. We're still a bit weird, and our clients seem to like us that way.</p>
<p>We move fast, priorities shift, it’s not always documented (whatever it is), our tool set and list of supported platforms is sprawling, and some days the to-do list is longer than you'd like. If you can stay warm under pressure, create order out of chaos, learn new stuff fast because you love it, and speak up when you need help, you'll thrive here.</p>
<p>If you need a rigid, fully-documented, never-changes environment where nobody trusts you with admin access, where you will be micro-managed so you never have to wonder whether or not you’re doing the right thing, this isn't it. You’ll have a ton of independence on our team, and freedom to use your best judgment to get the ball over the finish line, but that also means we expect you to be naturally responsible, honest, and highly accountable — to yourself, your teammates, and the clients. As they say, the price of freedom is eternal vigilance.</p>
<p><strong>The tech you'll work with (and grow into)</strong></p>
<p>Day one, you'll be living in:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Apple at the core — macOS, iOS, Apple Business Manager, and MDM (Jamf / Iru / Intune / Workspace ONE / and more)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Yes, PCs too — about one-third of our users are driving Windows</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 admin consoles</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Identity and access — SSO, MFA, and security keys (Okta, Entra ID, Yubikey, and friends)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Lots of other security tools — our stack is constantly evolving and may vary from client to client</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>The everyday productivity stack — Slack, Zoom, conference-room AV, networking basics (Meraki), and endpoint tooling</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>⠀ You don't need to know all of this. You need to be the kind of person who wants to. As you grow, there's a clear path into infrastructure administration, project architecture and implementation, and — for the right person — security and compliance work (phishing simulations, endpoint detection, SOC 2 / CIS / SEC readiness, due diligence questionnaire support, etc.). That's where our most senior people live, and it's wide open.</p>
<p><strong>Who we hope you are</strong></p>
<p>The hard requirements — we won't move forward unless you:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Can work standard business hours in Eastern or Pacific time zones (we currently need both)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Have a quiet home workspace and reliable broadband</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Have proven Apple aptitude — you actually use and like the Apple ecosystem</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Have at least 2 years of IT support experience on the job. Go-getters who are just getting started and can prove they have a deep, demonstrable history of teaching themselves technology for the sheer joy of it might also make the cut.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>⠀ Everything else — and this is the longer list, on purpose. We figure technical skills are teachable; character takes a lot longer. So we're looking for someone who:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Genuinely cares — about clients, teammates, and doing right by people</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Loves to learn, and proves it in how they spend their time</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Reads, and writes clearly — clear writing is clear thinking, and you'll do a lot of it</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Stays calm and kind with a frustrated person on the other end of the line</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Can tell the difference between good customer service and fantastic customer service, and refuses to settle for the former</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Notices when something's being done inefficiently and fixes it (you may boast about it later, that’s fine)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Proofreads their own work (yes, we noticed whether you proofread your application)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Has a sense of humor, and doesn't take themselves too seriously</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Regarding AI</strong></p>
<p>Our company founder/CEO is headquartered in the Bay Area. We work with innovative, ambitious companies developing AI products. Our team members hold a range of opinions about whether AI is “good or bad” for humans, but at the end of the day, we embrace the plain fact that it is here, we must embrace it to evolve, and our clients increasingly depend on us to help them embrace it, too, to help them evolve and stay competitive.</p>
<p>Our CEO's opinion: AI will not replace human labor any time soon; rather, AI is a new opportunity to either (a) get things done faster, or (b) get a much higher quality of output than before. If you’re an AI tinkerer, great! We want to hear how you’re using it and see your latest vibe project. If you’re allergic to AI or have no interest in learning new habits to work effectively with it, this is not the place for you.</p>
<p><strong>Compensation &amp; logistics</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Base salary: For applicants closer to the junior side who are located in the USA, $50,000–$65,000/yr, depending on experience level and your local geographical price index. With experience, comp goes up from there. Genuinely experienced, more senior candidates can be slotted higher — let's talk. Applicants outside the USA will be compensated according to your local employment market.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Type &amp; location: Full-time, work from home, fully remote. Eastern or Pacific time zones.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Benefits: Medical / dental / vision for the employee, 401(k) with employer contribution, 2 weeks of PTO annually to start, and a stipend for your home internet.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to apply</strong></p>
<p>The first step is really easy and only takes 10 minutes — no resume, no cover letter, no essays. Here's how:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>Take this online Culture Index assessment (about 10 minutes): We use it to screen for people whose natural wiring fits how our team works.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>If your profile lines up with what we're looking for, we'll reach out and ask you to complete a 60-minute TestGorilla assessment for critical thinking skills, send us your resume, and maybe a couple of other items.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>From there, we conduct between 2-4 video interviews. One of these might be a live technical assessment.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>If the Culture Index step feels like a strange way to start a job application — that's kind of the point. Remember the phrase Think Different?</p>
<p>Ignition is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome and will consider all qualified applicants. Good luck in your search — and may the Force be with you!</p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/ignition-inc-mac-msp-help-desk-guru-work-from-home">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/ignition-inc-mac-msp-help-desk-guru-work-from-home</a></p>
</description>

      <state>Florida</state>
      <skills>JavaScript and React</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
